text: Unit of pole strength of a magnet is
explanation:
Dipole moment of a magnet,

\( M = lA \Rightarrow 2ml = lA \quad [m = \text{pole strength}] \)

\( \Rightarrow m = \frac{lA}{2l} \)

Unit of pole strength \( (m) = \frac{\text{Ampere} \times \text{metre}^2}{\text{metre}} = \frac{\text{Am}^2}{\text{m}} = \text{Am} \).
name: Questions Item 1

text: Given figure shows the north and south poles of a permanent magnet in which a coil of n turns of cross-sectional area A is resting, such that when a current I is passed through the coil, the plane of the coil makes an angle θ with respect to direction of magnetic field B. If the plane of magnetic field and the coil are horizontal and vertical respectively, the torque on the coil will be
options: None of the above, since the magnetic field is radial
explanation:
Torque acting on the coil in magnetic field B is given as

\( \tau = nIAB \sin \theta \)

When the plane of magnetic field and the coil are horizontal and vertical respectively, then normal of coil makes 90° with the direction of magnetic field.

\( \therefore \theta = 90^{\circ} \)

\( \therefore \tau = nIAB \sin 90^{\circ} = nIAB \).
name: Questions Item 2

text: If the angles of dip at two places are 30° and 45° respectively, then the ratio of horizontal components of earth's magnetic field at the two places will be
explanation:
If B is the earth's net magnetic field, then the ratio of horizontal components of earth's magnetic field at the two places is given as

\( \frac{H_1}{H_2} = \frac{\cos \delta_1}{\cos \delta_2} = \frac{\cos 30^{\circ}}{\cos 45^{\circ}} = \frac{\sqrt{3}/2}{1/\sqrt{2}} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{2}} \)

\( \therefore H_1 : H_2 = \sqrt{3} : \sqrt{2} \).
name: Questions Item 3

text: The deflection produced in a tangent galvanometer, whose coil has a resistance of 9Ω is 30°. The potential difference across the coil is 4.5 V. If the number of turns in the coil is 10, the radius of the coil is (Given, \( B_H = 3.14 \times 10^{-5} \) T)
explanation:
Current flowing through the coil,

\( I = \frac{V}{R} = \frac{4.5}{9} = 0.5 \) A

\( I = \frac{2r B_H \tan\theta}{\mu_0 N} \)

\( \Rightarrow r = \frac{\mu_0 N I}{2 B_H \tan\theta} = \frac{4 \pi \times 10^{-7} \times 10 \times 0.5}{2 \times 3.14 \times 10^{-5} \times \tan 30^{\circ}} = 10\sqrt{3} \times 10^{-2} \) m.
name: Questions Item 4

text: A magnet of magnetic moment M is rotated through 360° in a magnetic field H, the work done will be
explanation:
Work done to rotate magnetic dipole in magnetic field from angle θ₁ to θ₂ is given as

\( W = MB(\cos\theta_1 - \cos\theta_2) \)

Here, θ₁ = 0° and θ₂ = 360°

\( \therefore W = MB(\cos 0^{\circ} - \cos 360^{\circ}) = MB(1 - 1) = 0 \).
name: Questions Item 5

text: A straight wire carrying current i is turned into a circular loop. If the magnitude of magnetic moment associated with it in MKS units is M, the length of wire will be
explanation:
Suppose, l be the length of wire.

Hence, l = 2πr ⇒ r = l / 2π

Magnetic dipole moment,

\( M = i \cdot \pi r^2 = i\pi \cdot (l / 2\pi)^2 = il^2 / 4\pi \)

\( \Rightarrow l^2 = 4M\pi / i \Rightarrow l = \sqrt{4\pi M / i} \).
name: Questions Item 6

text: Two identical thin bar magnets, each of length l and pole strength m, are placed at right angle to each other with north pole of one touching south pole of the other. The magnetic moment of the system is
explanation:
Magnetic dipole moment of each bar magnet

\( M_1 = M_2 = M = ml \)

M₁ and M₂ are perpendicular to each other.

\( \therefore \) Net magnetic moment of the system,

\( M = \sqrt{M_1^2 + M_2^2} = M\sqrt{2} = ml\sqrt{2} = \sqrt{2} \, ml \).
name: Questions Item 7

text: A magnetic needle lying parallel to a magnetic field requires W units of work to turn it through 60°. The torque required to maintain the needle in this position will be
explanation:
\( W = MB(\cos 0^{\circ} - \cos 60^{\circ}) = MB(1 - 1/2) = MB/2 \Rightarrow MB = 2W \)

\( \therefore \) Torque, \( \tau = MB \sin 60^{\circ} = 2W \times \sqrt{3}/2 = W\sqrt{3} = \sqrt{3}W \).
name: Questions Item 8

text: If the dipole moment of a short bar magnet is 1.25 A-m², find the magnetic field on its axis at a distance of 0.5 m from the centre of the magnet.
name: Questions Item 9

text: A circular parallel plate capacitor of radius R and spacing d is being charged by a constant current I₀. Find the magnetic field between the plates at a distance r from the axis, where r > R.
explanation:
Magnetic field at point P at a distance r (> R) due to current I₀ is given as

\( B = \frac{\mu_0}{2\pi} \cdot \frac{I_0}{r} \).
name: Questions Item 10

text: A bar magnet of length 10 cm and having the pole strength equal to 10^{-3} A-m is kept in a magnetic field having magnetic induction B equal to 4π × 10^{-3} T. It makes an angle of 30° with the direction of magnetic induction. The value of the torque acting on the magnet is
name: Questions Item 11

text: A thin rod of length L is magnetised and has a magnetic moment M. The rod is then bent in a semicircular arc. The magnetic moment in the new shape is
explanation:
\( M = m \times L \)

Now, rod is bent in form of a semi-circular arc,

\( M' = m \times (2R) = m \times 2 \times (L/\pi) = 2M/\pi \).
name: Questions Item 12

text: A metal rod is subjected to cycles of magnetisation at the rate of 42 Hz. Density of the metal is 6 × 10³ kg m⁻³ and its specific heat capacity is 0.1 × 10^{-3} cal kg⁻¹°C⁻¹. If the area of its B-H loop corresponds to energy density of 10^{-2} Jm⁻³, then the rise in its temperature in one minute is
name: Questions Item 13

text: The magnetic susceptibility of a paramagnetic substance at -173°C is 1.5 × 10^{-2}. To have the susceptibility 0.5 × 10^{-2}, the change in temperature in °C is
explanation:
\( T_1 = 100 \) K

According to Curie's law, \( \frac{\chi_{m1}}{\chi_{m2}} = \frac{T_2}{T_1} \)

\( T_2 = 300 \) K

\( \Delta T = 200^{\circ}\text{C} \).
name: Questions Item 14

text: Two short bar magnets A and B are arranged co-axially. The distance between their centres is 30 cm. A compass needle placed on their axis at a distance of 6 cm from B shows no deflection. The ratio of the magnetic moments of A and B is
name: Questions Item 15

text: The resultant magnetic moment of three magnetic dipoles, each of the magnetic moment M shown in the arrangement is
explanation:
\( M_1 = M\sqrt{2} \)

Resultant, \( M_{\text{resultant}} = M\sqrt{2} + M = (1 + \sqrt{2})M \).
name: Questions Item 16
